title: Introduction to SafeCleanR
text: SafeCleanR package contains simple tools : - cleaning missing values, - joining datasets, - creating summary statistics.

title: Introduction to SafeCleanR
code: student_data #>   student_id    name            major study_hours attendance final_grade #> 1        101   Alice Computer Science          15         90          88 #> 2        102     Bob          Biology          12         85          76 #> 3        103 Charlie        Economics          NA         88          91 #> 4        104   David      Mathematics          20         NA          85 #> 5        105    Emma          Physics          18         95          NA #> 6        106   Frank          History          NA         80          73 #> 7        107   Grace     Data Science          25         92          96 #> 8        108   Henry        Chemistry          10         NA          81

title: Introduction to SafeCleanR
text: Replace missing values mean: Remove rows missing values:
code: clean_missing(student_data, "study_hours", method = "mean") #>   student_id    name            major study_hours attendance final_grade #> 1        101   Alice Computer Science    15.00000         90          88 #> 2        102     Bob          Biology    12.00000         85          76 #> 3        103 Charlie        Economics    16.66667         88          91 #> 4        104   David      Mathematics    20.00000         NA          85 #> 5        105    Emma          Physics    18.00000         95          NA #> 6        106   Frank          History    16.66667         80          73 #> 7        107   Grace     Data Science    25.00000         92          96 #> 8        108   Henry        Chemistry    10.00000         NA          81 clean_missing(student_data, "final_grade", method = "remove") #>   student_id    name            major study_hours attendance final_grade #> 1        101   Alice Computer Science          15         90          88 #> 2        102     Bob          Biology          12         85          76 #> 3        103 Charlie        Economics          NA         88          91 #> 4        104   David      Mathematics          20         NA          85 #> 6        106   Frank          History          NA         80          73 #> 7        107   Grace     Data Science          25         92          96 #> 8        108   Henry        Chemistry          10         NA          81

title: Introduction to SafeCleanR
text: Create another dataset: Join datasets:
code: advisor_data <- data.frame(   student_id = c(101, 102, 103, 104),   advisor = c(     "Dr. Smith",     "Dr. Lee",     "Dr. Brown",     "Dr. Patel"   ) ) safe_join(student_data, advisor_data, by = "student_id") #> Warning in safe_join(student_data, advisor_data, by = "student_id"): 4 row(s) #> in df1 have no match in df2 #>   student_id    name            major study_hours attendance final_grade #> 1        101   Alice Computer Science          15         90          88 #> 2        102     Bob          Biology          12         85          76 #> 3        103 Charlie        Economics          NA         88          91 #> 4        104   David      Mathematics          20         NA          85 #> 5        105    Emma          Physics          18         95          NA #> 6        106   Frank          History          NA         80          73 #> 7        107   Grace     Data Science          25         92          96 #> 8        108   Henry        Chemistry          10         NA          81 #>     advisor #> 1 Dr. Smith #> 2   Dr. Lee #> 3 Dr. Brown #> 4 Dr. Patel #> 5      <NA> #> 6      <NA> #> 7      <NA> #> 8      <NA>

what: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: safecleanr R package provides simple safe tools cleaning summarizing data. package focuses beginner-friendly workflows : - handling missing values, - safely joining datasets, - generating quick summary statistics.

title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: can install development version GitHub:

title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: Handles missing values using: - mean replacement, - median replacement, - mode replacement, - row removal. Example:
code: library(safecleanr)  df <- data.frame(   age = c(20, NA, 25, 30) )  clean_missing(df, "age", method = "mean")

title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: Safely joins two data frames checking : - duplicate keys, - missing join columns, - unmatched rows. Example:
code: df1 <- data.frame(   id = c(1, 2, 3),   name = c("Alice", "Bob", "Charlie") )  df2 <- data.frame(   id = c(1, 2),   score = c(90, 85) )  safe_join(df1, df2, by = "id")

title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: Generates summary statistics numeric columns. Example:

title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: package includes sample dataset called student_data. dataset contains: - student IDs, - majors, - study hours, - attendance, - final grades.

title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
code: library(safecleanr)  # View dataset student_data  # Clean missing values cleaned_data <- clean_missing(   student_data,   "study_hours",   method = "mean" )  # Generate summary statistics quick_summary(cleaned_data)

title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: package created provide safer simpler data-cleaning tools beginner R users. Many common data-cleaning tasks can silently introduce mistakes confusion. functions package help users identify problems earlier work data confidently.

title: Safely Join Two Data Frame Performs a left join with safety checks for: duplicate keys, missing join columns, and unmatched rows — safe_join
code: df1 <- data.frame(id = c(1, 2, 3), name = c("A", "B", "C")) df2 <- data.frame(id = c(1, 2, 4), score = c(90, 85, 70))  safe_join(df1, df2, by = "id") #> Warning: 1 row(s) in df1 have no match in df2 #>   id name score #> 1  1    A    90 #> 2  2    B    85 #> 3  3    C    NA
